methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $9M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Orbital Operations is a Seed-stage space & aerospace company founded by Ben Schleuniger and Ross Doherty that has raised $8.8M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comparables including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though the density of exact-stage matches within this sector remains limited.

Of three valuation methods tested, only stage-progression proves applicable. Using the $8.8M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next-stage funding, this method yields $22M. Peer-median triangulation is not feasible due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Space & Aerospace sector; expanding the filter would sacrifice relevance. Public-comp anchoring cannot proceed because Orbital Operations does not disclose revenue—a prerequisite for applying the 59.8x median P/S multiple observed across the 5 public tickers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $19M to $25M, centered on $22M. This range reflects a single robust methodological input and carries material uncertainty around future funding velocity, competitive positioning within commercial space, and whether the company achieves measurable technical or commercial milestones prior to Series A. A revenue-generating milestone or identifiable Series A lead would materially strengthen valuation support.
